            # blueprint-writer bw257-eng — fix `Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex` (finiteness bridge + false marker)

Edit ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`. Three corrections.

## Correction 1 (MUST-FIX) — add the finiteness bridge to the `chartPresentation` / `isFinitePresentation` proof sketches

The Lean main theorem `isFinitePresentation` needs `(chartPresentation M U e).IsFinite` to build a
`SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation`. The current chapter says "transport the
presentation along the trivialisation" / "feed to `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`" but does NOT
name the bridge. Add the concrete recipe (verified against Mathlib `…/SheafOfModules/Quasicoherent.lean`):

- In the proof sketch of `lem:lbc_chart_presentation`: build `chartPresentation M U e` by transporting the
  canonical finite free presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf` along the
  trivialisation iso, via `SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso e.hom` (Mathlib). State that the Mathlib
  instance making `(ofIsIso f σ).IsFinite` hold when the source presentation `σ.IsFinite` holds means the
  result's `IsFinite` is inferred automatically by typeclass resolution — so NO separate 6th declaration
  `chartPresentation_isFinite` is required; the finiteness rides along as an instance on the `ofIsIso`
  construction. (Confirm/State that the canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit R` is finite — it
  is the rank-one free presentation — citing the Mathlib unit-presentation instance.)
- In the proof sketch of `thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation`: assemble the `QuasicoherentData` from the
  trivialising cover (`exists_trivializing_cover`) with `chartPresentation` at each chart, note that each
  chart's `IsFinite` is the automatic instance from `ofIsIso`, and invoke
  `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk` (or the anonymous `⟨⟨…⟩⟩` constructor) on that data. The point
  the prover needs: the `IsFinite` obligation is discharged by inference, not a separate lemma.

## Correction 2 (MUST-FIX) — remove the false proof-block `\leanok`

The proof block of `lem:lbc_chart_presentation` currently carries a `\leanok` (manually inserted in error
when the chapter was written), but `chartPresentation` is an iter-256 `sorry` stub. Per the
blueprint-reviewer (br257) this is a purity violation: a proof-block `\leanok` asserts the proof is closed,
which is false. REMOVE that single erroneous `\leanok` from the `chartPresentation` proof block so the
chapter reflects the true (unproved) state. (`\leanok` is otherwise sync-managed; this is a one-off removal
of a demonstrably-false manual marker, explicitly requested by the reviewer — do NOT add any new markers.)

## Correction 3 (minor) — soften two overclaims

- `cor:lbc_isFiniteType`: the prose claims both `IsFiniteType` AND `IsQuasicoherent`; the Lean states only
  `IsFiniteType`. Soften to: `IsFiniteType` is the named result; `IsQuasicoherent` follows automatically
  from `isFinitePresentation` via a Mathlib instance (no separate named theorem needed).
- `lem:lbc_rank_flat` (`chart_free_rank_one`): the prose claims "free of rank one, in particular flat", but
  the Lean type only records the chart-local iso `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` (Mathlib has no SheafOfModules-level
  rank/flat predicate). Keep the statement, but make the prose honest: this records the rank-one free
  MODEL via the iso to `unit`; rank-one freeness and flatness are consequences of that iso, not separately
  stated in the type. (This keeps it from reading as a stronger claim than the type supports.)

## Out of scope
Do NOT edit any other chapter. Keep the verbatim Stacks `% SOURCE QUOTE` citation blocks intact. Do NOT
add any `\leanok`/`\mathlibok`.

1	\chapter{Coherence of locally trivial line bundles}
2	\label{chap:Picard_LineBundleCoherence}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundleCoherence.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter supplies the cheap, on-path half of the
6	% line-bundle coherence requirement of the A.2.c Quot-scheme embedding
7	% (\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}).
8	% The Quot functor takes coherent quotients of a coherent sheaf; to feed it a
9	% line bundle one must know that the line bundle is itself coherent, i.e. a
10	% finitely presented \(\mathcal{O}\)-module. The embedding's input is a point of
11	% the relative Picard scheme \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\), whose carrier is the
12	% \emph{locally trivial} predicate \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) of
13	% \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (the project-side stand-in for an
14	% invertible sheaf, a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules locally isomorphic to
15	% \(\mathcal{O}\) of rank one). The chapter proves
16	% \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} M \Rightarrow M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\),
17	% together with a rank-one / flatness record. The general implication
18	% ``\(M\) tensor-invertible \(\Rightarrow\) \(M\) locally free of rank one''
19	% (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma~0B8M when stalks are local) is
20	% \emph{not} crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
21	% finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.
22	
23	\section{Setup and motivation}
24	\label{sec:lbc_setup}
25	
26	Fix a scheme \(X\) and write \(X.\mathtt{Modules}\) for the category of sheaves
27	of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) on the
28	ringed-space site of \(X\), with structure sheaf \(X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)).
29	A \emph{line bundle} on \(X\) is an invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module; in the
30	sense of \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} we model line bundles by the
31	predicate \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}\): a module \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) is locally
32	trivial of rank one if every point \(x \in X\) has an \emph{affine} open
33	neighbourhood \(U\) on which the restriction \(M|_U\) is isomorphic, as an
34	\(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, to the structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U}\):
35	\[
36	  \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M) \;:=\;
37	  \forall x \in X,\ \exists\, U \ni x \text{ affine open},\quad
38	  M|_U \;\cong\; \struct{U}.
39	\]
40	This is the rank-one case of Stacks Definition~17.25.1
41	(Tag~01CS, invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules), and
42	agrees with the classical ``locally free of rank one'' description by Stacks
43	Lemma~17.25.4 (Tag~0B8M).
44	
45	The goal of the chapter is to record that such a module is \emph{coherent} in the
46	sense the Quot construction consumes, namely finitely presented:
47	\(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) in Mathlib's
48	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\) (built from a
49	\(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\)). The mathematics is elementary: on each
50	trivialising chart \(M|_U \cong \struct{U}\) is finite free of rank one, hence
51	has the trivial finite presentation, and finite presentation is by definition a
52	local condition. No stalk computation and no spreading-out are involved. The
53	content is the bookkeeping that turns the pointwise trivialisation into the
54	indexed \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\) Mathlib expects.
55	
56	\section{Site instances for quasi-coherent data}
57	\label{sec:lbc_site_instances}
58	
59	% NOTE (chief unknown). Mathlib's SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation /
60	% QuasicoherentData are stated under three site hypotheses on the slice
61	% topologies J.over X of the underlying site J of X.ringCatSheaf:
62	%   [∀ x, (J.over x).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
63	%   [∀ x, HasWeakSheafify (J.over x) AddCommGrpCat]
64	%   [∀ x, (J.over x).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
65	% These must be discharged (or assumed as instances) before the statements in
66	% this chapter typecheck for X.ringCatSheaf. The project already lives in the
67	% SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf world, so these should resolve; this is the one
68	% item to verify when the Lean file is scaffolded.
69	
70	The Mathlib predicates \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\) and
71	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData}\) are stated relative to a
72	Grothendieck topology \(J\) on a base category \(C\) and a sheaf of rings
73	\(R : \Sheaf(J, \RingCat)\). They require, for every object \(x : C\), three
74	instances on the slice topology \(J/x\) (Mathlib \(J.\mathtt{over}\ x\)):
75	\[
76	  (J/x).\mathtt{HasSheafCompose}(\forget_{\RingCat \to \AddCommGrpCat}),\quad
77	  \HasWeakSheafify(J/x, \AddCommGrpCat),\quad
78	  (J/x).\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}(\AddCommGrpCat).
79	\]
80	For \(X.\mathtt{Modules} = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\ X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
81	these are the ambient site instances of the ringed space underlying \(X\). We
82	record their availability as a standing assumption for the chapter; verifying it
83	for \(X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is the single instance-resolution check the
84	formalisation must clear at entry, and is independent of the mathematical
85	content below.
86	
87	\begin{remark}
88	  \label{rem:lbc_site_instances}
89	  For the ringed-space site underlying \(X\) with sheaf of rings
90	  \(R = X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\), every slice topology \(J/x\) carries the
91	  three instances \(\mathtt{HasSheafCompose}\), \(\HasWeakSheafify\) and
92	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) (for the forgetful functor
93	  \(\RingCat \to \AddCommGrpCat\)) demanded by
94	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData}\). Under this assumption the
95	  predicate \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) is well-formed for every
96	  \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\).
97	\end{remark}
98	
99	\section{From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover}
100	\label{sec:lbc_cover}
101	
102	\begin{lemma}\leanok
103	[Trivialising affine cover]
104	  \label{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
105	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover}
106	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product}
107	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Definition 17.25.1
108	  % "invertible module" (Tag 01CS), Section 17.25 (Tag 01CR)
109	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4059).
110	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
111	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
112	  %  {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
113	  %  of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that
114	  %  the functor
115	  %  $$
116	  %  \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \longrightarrow \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X),\quad
117	  %  \mathcal{F} \longmapsto \mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}
118	  %  $$
119	  %  is an equivalence of categories. We say that $\mathcal{L}$ is
120	  %  {\it trivial} if it is isomorphic as an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
121	  %  to $\mathcal{O}_X$."
122	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CS (Modules, Definition~17.25.1).}
123	  Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial, \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\).
124	  Then there exist an index set \(I\), a family of affine open subschemes
125	  \(\{U_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(X\) covering \(X\) (i.e. \(\bigcup_i U_i = X\)), and
126	  for each \(i\) an isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
127	  \[
128	    e_i \;:\; M|_{U_i} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \struct{U_i}
129	  \]
130	  to the structure sheaf-as-module on \(U_i\).
131	\end{lemma}
132	
133	\begin{proof}
134	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product}
135	  Apply the hypothesis \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\) at every point \(x \in X\): it
136	  produces an affine open \(U_x \ni x\) and an isomorphism
137	  \(e_x : M|_{U_x} \cong \struct{U_x}\). Take the index set \(I := X\) (the
138	  underlying set of points), and to the index \(x\) assign the chosen
139	  neighbourhood \(U_x\) and the chosen isomorphism \(e_x\). The family
140	  \(\{U_x\}_{x \in X}\) covers \(X\) because each \(x\) lies in its own \(U_x\).
141	  This is exactly the data the definition supplies, packaged as an indexed
142	  family rather than a pointwise existential.
143	\end{proof}
144	
145	\section{The trivial presentation on a chart}
146	\label{sec:lbc_chart_presentation}
147	
148	\begin{lemma}\leanok
149	[Finite free presentation on a chart]
150	  \label{lem:lbc_chart_presentation}
151	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation}
152	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
153	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, section
154	  % "Modules of finite presentation" (\label{definition-finite-presentation})
155	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L1377-L1392).
156	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
157	  % "We say that $\mathcal{F}$ is of {\it finite presentation}
158	  %  if for every point $x \in X$ there exists an open neighbourhood
159	  %  $x\in U \subset X$, and  $n, m \in \mathbf{N}$ such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$
160	  %  is isomorphic to the cokernel of a map
161	  %  $$
162	  %  \bigoplus\nolimits_{j = 1, \ldots, m}
163	  %  \mathcal{O}_U
164	  %  \longrightarrow
165	  %  \bigoplus\nolimits_{i = 1, \ldots, n}
166	  %  \mathcal{O}_U
167	  %  $$"
168	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules, ``Modules of finite
169	  presentation''.}
170	  On each chart \(U_i\) of the trivialising cover of
171	  \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}, the restriction \(M|_{U_i}\) admits the
172	  trivial finite free presentation
173	  \[
174	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \struct{U_i} \;\longrightarrow\; M|_{U_i}
175	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
176	  \]
177	  i.e. \(M|_{U_i}\) is the cokernel of the zero map
178	  \(\bigoplus_{j \in \varnothing} \struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\)
179	  (the case \(m = 0\), \(n = 1\) of the finite-presentation pattern). The
180	  unit sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i}\) (Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\))
181	  carries a canonical free presentation on one generator with no relations;
182	  this presentation is finite.
183	\end{lemma}
184	
185	\begin{proof}
186	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
187	  \leanok
188	  The structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one on the
189	  single generator \(1\): the identity map
190	  \(\struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\) exhibits it as
191	  \(\bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), so the surjection
192	  \(\struct{U_i}^{\oplus 1} \twoheadrightarrow \struct{U_i}\) is an isomorphism
193	  and its kernel is zero, generated by the empty family. Thus
194	  \(\struct{U_i}\) has a presentation with \(n = 1\) generators and \(m = 0\)
195	  relations; this presentation is finite. Transporting it along the
196	  trivialisation \(e_i : M|_{U_i} \xrightarrow{\sim} \struct{U_i}\) of
197	  \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} (presentations are stable under
198	  isomorphism of the presented module) yields a finite free presentation of
199	  \(M|_{U_i}\).
200	\end{proof}
201	
202	\section{Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle}
203	\label{sec:lbc_finite_presentation}
204	
205	\begin{theorem}\leanok
206	[Locally trivial \(\Rightarrow\) finitely presented]
207	  \label{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
208	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation}
209	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover, lem:lbc_chart_presentation, rem:lbc_site_instances}
210	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces,
211	  % Lemma 17.25.4 (Tag 0B8M), invertible vs locally free of rank one
212	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4159-L4165).
213	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
214	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Any locally free
215	  %  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module of rank $1$ is invertible.
216	  %  If all stalks $\mathcal{O}_{X, x}$ are local rings, then
217	  %  the converse holds as well (but in general this is not the case)."
218	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~0B8M (Modules, Lemma~17.25.4).}
219	  Let \(X\) be a scheme satisfying \cref{rem:lbc_site_instances} and let
220	  \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial, \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\). Then
221	  \(M\) is finitely presented: \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) holds in
222	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\).
223	\end{theorem}
224	
225	\begin{proof}
226	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover, lem:lbc_chart_presentation}
227	  By \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} choose a trivialising affine cover
228	  \(\{U_i\}_{i \in I}\) with isomorphisms \(e_i : M|_{U_i} \cong \struct{U_i}\).
229	  Assemble these into a \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\) for \(M\): the index type
230	  is \(I\), the covering objects are the affine opens \(U_i\) (which cover \(X\)),
231	  and the presentation attached to index \(i\) is the finite free presentation of
232	  \(M|_{U_i}\) (equivalently, of the restriction \(M.\mathtt{over}\,U_i\))
233	  produced in \cref{lem:lbc_chart_presentation}. Each such presentation is
234	  finite, so the resulting quasi-coherent datum is a
235	  \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation}\). Feeding it to
236	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk}\) gives
237	  \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\).
238	
239	  The only non-formal input is the trivialising cover; everything else is the
240	  definitional unwinding of finite presentation as a local property
241	  (Stacks, ``Modules of finite presentation''), since a rank-one free module is its own finite
242	  presentation. No stalk computation and no neighbourhood-spreading argument is
243	  used: the hypothesis already provides the local free models.
244	\end{proof}
245	
246	\begin{corollary}\leanok
247	[Locally trivial \(\Rightarrow\) finite type and quasi-coherent]
248	  \label{cor:lbc_isFiniteType}
249	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType}
250	  \uses{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
251	  Under the hypotheses of \cref{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}, the locally
252	  trivial module \(M\) is also of finite type
253	  (\(M.\mathtt{IsFiniteType}\)) and quasi-coherent
254	  (\(M.\mathtt{IsQuasicoherent}\)).
255	\end{corollary}
256	
257	\begin{proof}
258	  \uses{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
259	  Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
260	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation}\), and implies
261	  quasi-coherence by
262	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation}\); both are
263	  immediate from \cref{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}.
264	\end{proof}
265	
266	\section{Rank one and flatness record}
267	\label{sec:lbc_rank_flat}
268	
269	% NOTE. Mathlib (at the pin) has no SheafOfModules-level "locally free of rank r"
270	% or "flat" predicate (verified absent: no SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFree). The
271	% rank-one and flatness facts are therefore recorded chart-locally, as the data
272	% the Quot embedding actually consumes, rather than as instances of a missing
273	% global predicate. Project-side predicates may be introduced if a downstream
274	% consumer needs them packaged; this is flagged for the plan agent.
275	
276	\begin{lemma}\leanok
277	[Chart-local rank one and flatness]
278	  \label{lem:lbc_rank_flat}
279	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one}
280	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
281	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, section
282	  % "Locally free sheaves" (\label{definition-locally-free}, "(finite)
283	  % locally free of rank $r$")
284	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2079-L2094).
285	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
286	  % "\item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it locally free} if for every
287	  %  point $x \in X$ there exist a set $I$ and an open
288	  %  neighbourhood $x \in U \subset X$
289	  %  such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to
290	  %  $\bigoplus_{i \in I} \mathcal{O}_X|_U$ as an $\mathcal{O}_X|_U$-module.
291	  %  \item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it finite locally free} if we may
292	  %  choose the index sets $I$ to be finite.
293	  %  \item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it finite locally free of rank $r$}
294	  %  if we may choose the index sets $I$ to have cardinality $r$."
295	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules, ``Locally free sheaves'' (finite
296	  locally free of rank \(r\)).}
297	  Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial. On each chart \(U_i\) of the
298	  trivialising cover of \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} the restriction
299	  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one over
300	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); in particular it is flat, because \(\struct{U_i}\) is a
301	  flat \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-module (a ring is a flat module over itself, and
302	  flatness is preserved by the isomorphism \(e_i\)). Consequently \(M\) is, in
303	  the sense of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, finite locally free of rank one
304	  and flat: these properties hold on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) by construction.
305	\end{lemma}
306	
307	\begin{proof}
308	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
309	  Immediate from the trivialisation: on \(U_i\), \(e_i\) identifies \(M|_{U_i}\)
310	  with \(\struct{U_i} = \bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), the free module
311	  of rank one. The structure sheaf-as-module is flat over itself, and an
312	  isomorphic module is flat; the index set \(\{*\}\) has cardinality one, so the
313	  rank is one. The properties are stated on the chart \(U_i\) because there is no
314	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)-level locally free or flat predicate globally; the chart-local statements are
315	  what the Quot-scheme embedding consumes when it forms the Hilbert-polynomial
316	  input on a fibre.
317	\end{proof}
318
I'll make the three corrections. Let me first check the project CLAUDE.md and the Lean file briefly to ground the claims, then edit.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.LineBundlePullback
7	
8	/-!
9	# Coherence of locally trivial line bundles (A.2.c engine)
10	
11	This file is the **iter-256 Lane engine** file-skeleton for the cheap, on-path
12	half of the line-bundle coherence requirement of the A.2.c Quot-scheme
13	embedding (`chap:Picard_QuotScheme`, `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`).
14	
15	The Quot functor takes coherent quotients of a coherent sheaf; to feed it a line
16	bundle one must know that the line bundle is itself coherent, i.e. a finitely
17	presented `𝒪`-module. The embedding's input is a point of the relative Picard
18	scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, whose carrier is the **locally trivial** predicate
19	`IsLocallyTrivial` of `chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback` (the project-side stand-in
20	for an invertible sheaf). The chapter proves
21	`IsLocallyTrivial M ⟹ M.IsFinitePresentation`, together with a rank-one /
22	flatness record. The general implication "`M` tensor-invertible `⟹` `M` locally
23	free of rank one" (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma 0B8M when stalks are
24	local) is *not* crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
25	finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.
26	
27	## Status (iter-256 file-skeleton)
28	
29	This file is the **iter-256** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned declarations
30	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the blueprint
31	`\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`) with a `sorry`
32	body. The bodies are iter-257+ work, contingent on the site-instance de-risk
33	report below.
34	
35	The 5 pinned declarations are:
36	
37	1. `IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover` (`lem:lbc_trivializing_cover`) —
38	   repackage the pointwise existential of `IsLocallyTrivial` into an indexed
39	   trivialising affine cover.
40	2. `IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation` (`lem:lbc_chart_presentation`) — the
41	   trivial finite free presentation of `M.over U` on a trivialising chart.
42	3. `IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation` (`thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation`) —
43	   the main theorem: assemble a `QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising cover
44	   and feed it to `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`.
45	4. `IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType` (`cor:lbc_isFiniteType`) — finite type +
46	   quasi-coherence, immediate from finite presentation.
47	5. `IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one` (`lem:lbc_rank_flat`) — the chart-local
48	   rank-one free record.
49	
50	## Site instances (the chief de-risk; see `rem:lbc_site_instances`)
51	
52	Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation` / `QuasicoherentData` are stated
53	under three site hypotheses on the slice topologies `J.over U` of the underlying
54	topology `J = Opens.grothendieckTopology X` of `X.ringCatSheaf`:
55	
56	```
57	[∀ U, (J.over U).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
58	[∀ U, HasWeakSheafify (J.over U) AddCommGrpCat]     -- (resp. HasSheafify)
59	[∀ U, (J.over U).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
60	```
61	
62	The de-risk outcome (whether these resolve for `X.ringCatSheaf`) is recorded in
63	the task result and probed by the `#check` commands at the end of this file.
64	
65	## References
66	
67	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`.
68	Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Definition 17.25.1
69	(Tag 01CS, invertible modules), "Modules of finite presentation", and
70	Lemma 17.25.4 (Tag 0B8M).
71	-/
72	
73	set_option autoImplicit false
74	
75	universe u
76	
77	open CategoryTheory Limits
78	
79	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
80	
81	namespace Scheme
82	
83	namespace LineBundle
84	
85	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
86	
87	/-! ## §1. From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover -/
88	
89	/-- **Trivialising affine cover** (Stacks 01CS / Definition 17.25.1).
90	A locally trivial module `M` admits an indexed trivialising affine cover: an
91	index type `I`, a family of affine opens `U i` covering `X`, and for each `i` a
92	`𝒪_{U_i}`-module isomorphism `M|_{U_i} ≅ 𝒪_{U_i}`.
93	
94	The witness repackages the pointwise existential `IsLocallyTrivial M` (take
95	`I := X` and assign each point its chosen neighbourhood). -/
96	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover {M : X.Modules}
97	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
98	    ∃ (I : Type u) (U : I → X.Opens),
99	      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ iSup U = ⊤ ∧
100	        ∀ i, Nonempty (M.restrict (U i).ι ≅
101	          SheafOfModules.unit (U i : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
102	  sorry
103	
104	/-! ## §2. The trivial presentation on a chart -/
105	
106	/-- **Finite free presentation on a chart** (Stacks, "Modules of finite
107	presentation"). On a trivialising chart `U` — i.e. with an isomorphism
108	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` — the restriction `M.over U` admits the trivial finite free
109	presentation (one generator, no relations), obtained by transporting the
110	canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit` along the trivialisation.
111	
112	The body bridges the scheme-level restriction `M.restrict U.ι` (carrier of the
113	trivialisation) with the slice-site restriction `M.over U` (what
114	`QuasicoherentData` consumes), then transports `unit`'s rank-one free
115	presentation. -/
116	noncomputable def IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
117	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
118	    (M.over U).Presentation :=
119	  sorry
120	
121	/-! ## §3. Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle -/
122	
123	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finitely presented** (Stacks 0B8M / Lemma 17.25.4).
124	A locally trivial module on a scheme is finitely presented: assemble a
125	`QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising affine cover
126	(`exists_trivializing_cover`), whose presentation at each index is the finite
127	free presentation of `chartPresentation`, and feed it to
128	`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`. No stalk computation or
129	neighbourhood-spreading is involved. -/
130	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation {M : X.Modules}
131	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
132	    M.IsFinitePresentation := by
133	  sorry
134	
135	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finite type and quasi-coherent** (corollary of
136	`isFinitePresentation`). Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
137	`SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation` and quasi-coherence by
138	`SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`. -/
139	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType {M : X.Modules}
140	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
141	    M.IsFiniteType := by
142	  sorry
143	
144	/-! ## §4. Rank one and flatness record -/
145	
146	/-- **Chart-local rank one and flatness** (Stacks, "Locally free sheaves":
147	finite locally free of rank `r`). On a trivialising chart the restriction
148	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` is free of rank one over `𝒪_U`; in particular flat (`𝒪_U` is a flat
149	module over itself, and flatness is preserved by the isomorphism). The fact is
150	recorded chart-locally — as the rank-one free model `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U = ⊕_{*} 𝒪_U`
151	the Quot embedding consumes — because Mathlib has no `SheafOfModules`-level
152	locally-free / flat / rank predicate. -/
153	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one {M : X.Modules}
154	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) (x : X) :
155	    ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
156	      Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
157	  sorry
158	
159	end LineBundle
160	
161	end Scheme
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry

Now I have enough context. Let me apply the three corrections.

**Correction 1a + Correction 2**: Rewrite the `lem:lbc_chart_presentation` proof block — remove the false `\leanok` and add the `ofIsIso` finiteness bridge.

input.old_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
  \leanok
  The structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one on the
  single generator \(1\): the identity map
  \(\struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\) exhibits it as
  \(\bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), so the surjection
  \(\struct{U_i}^{\oplus 1} \twoheadrightarrow \struct{U_i}\) is an isomorphism
  and its kernel is zero, generated by the empty family. Thus
  \(\struct{U_i}\) has a presentation with \(n = 1\) generators and \(m = 0\)
  relations; this presentation is finite. Transporting it along the
  trivialisation \(e_i : M|_{U_i} \xrightarrow{\sim} \struct{U_i}\) of
  \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} (presentations are stable under
  isomorphism of the presented module) yields a finite free presentation of
  \(M|_{U_i}\).
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
  The structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i} = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,
  (U_i).\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is free of rank one on the single generator
  \(1\): the identity map \(\struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\) exhibits it as
  \(\bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), so the surjection
  \(\struct{U_i}^{\oplus 1} \twoheadrightarrow \struct{U_i}\) is an isomorphism
  and its kernel is zero, generated by the empty family. Thus \(\struct{U_i}\)
  has the canonical presentation with \(n = 1\) generators and \(m = 0\)
  relations; this canonical \(\mathtt{unit}\)-presentation is the rank-one free
  presentation, and Mathlib records it as \emph{finite} (the
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\) finite-presentation instance).

  Concretely, in Lean the chart presentation is built by \emph{transport along
  the trivialisation iso}: take the canonical presentation \(\sigma\) of
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,(U_i).\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) and apply
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso}\,e_i.\mathtt{hom}\) (Mathlib's
  transport of a presentation along an isomorphism \(e_i.\mathtt{hom}\) of the
  presented module) to obtain \((M.\mathtt{over}\,U_i).\mathtt{Presentation}\).
  Crucially, Mathlib supplies the instance making
  \((\mathtt{ofIsIso}\,f\,\sigma).\mathtt{IsFinite}\) hold whenever
  \(\sigma.\mathtt{IsFinite}\) holds; since the canonical \(\mathtt{unit}\)
  presentation is finite, the finiteness of the transported presentation is
  \emph{inferred automatically by typeclass resolution} on the
  \(\mathtt{ofIsIso}\) construction. No separate finiteness declaration
  (e.g.\ a \(\mathtt{chartPresentation\_isFinite}\) lemma) is needed: the
  \(\mathtt{IsFinite}\) datum rides along as an instance on
  \(\mathtt{chartPresentation}\) itself.
\end{proof}

produced in \cref{lem:lbc_chart_presentation}. Each such presentation's

  automatic typeclass instance riding along on the

**Correction 3b**: Make the `lem:lbc_rank_flat` prose honest — the Lean type only records the chart-local iso.

input.old_string:
Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial. On each chart \(U_i\) of the
  trivialising cover of \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} the restriction
  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one over
  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); in particular it is flat, because \(\struct{U_i}\) is a
  flat \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-module (a ring is a flat module over itself, and
  flatness is preserved by the isomorphism \(e_i\)). Consequently \(M\) is, in
  the sense of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, finite locally free of rank one
  and flat: these properties hold on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) by construction.
\end{lemma}
input.new_string:
Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial. The lemma records, at each
  point \(x \in X\), a chart \(U \ni x\) together with the trivialising iso
  \(M|_U \cong \struct{U}\) (to \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\)). This iso is the
  rank-one free \emph{model}: the Lean type states only the iso itself, since
  Mathlib has no \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)-level rank or flatness predicate.
  Rank-one freeness and flatness are then \emph{consequences} of this iso — not
  separately recorded in the type — because \(\struct{U}\) is the free
  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module of rank one and a flat module over itself (a ring is
  flat over itself), and both properties transport along \(e\). In the informal
  sense of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product} this exhibits \(M\) as finite
  locally free of rank one and flat on the cover, but the formal content is
  exactly the chart-local iso.
\end{lemma}

# blueprint-writer bw257-eng — report

Edited ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`. All three directed corrections applied.

## Correction 1 (MUST-FIX) — finiteness bridge named in both proof sketches — DONE

- **`lem:lbc_chart_presentation` proof block** (now L185–213): added the concrete recipe. The chart
  presentation is built by transporting the canonical finite free presentation `σ` of
  `SheafOfModules.unit (U_i).ringCatSheaf` along the trivialisation iso, via
  `SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso e_i.hom`. Stated explicitly that the Mathlib instance making
  `(ofIsIso f σ).IsFinite` hold when `σ.IsFinite` holds means the result's `IsFinite` is inferred by
  typeclass resolution — so NO separate 6th declaration `chartPresentation_isFinite` is required; the
  `IsFinite` datum rides along as an instance on the `ofIsIso`/`chartPresentation` construction.
  Confirmed in prose that the canonical `SheafOfModules.unit` presentation is the rank-one free
  presentation and is finite (citing the unit finite-presentation instance).
- **`thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation` proof block** (now ~L235–244): noted each chart's `IsFinite` is the
  automatic `ofIsIso` instance from `chartPresentation` (not supplied by hand), so the
  `QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation` finiteness obligation is discharged by inference; then invoke
  `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk` (equivalently the anonymous `⟨⟨…⟩⟩` constructor). The point the
  prover needs — `IsFinite` discharged by inference, not a separate lemma — is now explicit in both places.

## Correction 2 (MUST-FIX) — false proof-block `\leanok` removed — DONE

Removed the single erroneous `\leanok` from the `lem:lbc_chart_presentation` proof block (was on the old
line 187). `chartPresentation` is an iter-256 `sorry` stub, so the marker was a purity violation. No new
markers added anywhere. The `\leanok` on the lemma *statement* line (L148) was left untouched (statement
markers are sync-managed and out of scope of the reviewer's one-off request).

## Correction 3 (minor) — two overclaims softened — DONE

- **`cor:lbc_isFiniteType`**: retitled to "Locally trivial ⟹ finite type"; prose now states `IsFiniteType`
  is the named result and that `IsQuasicoherent` follows automatically via the Mathlib instance
  `SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation` (no separate named theorem). Proof block
  updated to match: quasi-coherence is "not separately stated in the Lean type" but available as the
  instance.
- **`lem:lbc_rank_flat`** (`chart_free_rank_one`): statement kept; prose made honest — the Lean type records
  only the chart-local iso `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` (to `unit`), which is the rank-one free *model*; rank-one freeness
  and flatness are *consequences* of that iso, not separately stated in the type (Mathlib has no
  SheafOfModules-level rank/flat predicate). No longer reads as a stronger claim than the type supports.

## Out of scope — respected

- No other chapter edited.
- Verbatim Stacks `% SOURCE QUOTE` citation blocks left intact.
- No new `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` added (Correction 2 only removed one).

## Verification notes

- Bridge names (`SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso`, `SheafOfModules.unit`, `IsFinitePresentation.mk`,
  `instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation`, `instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`) match the names
  already used by the Lean skeleton `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundleCoherence.lean` docstrings and the
  directive. `M.over U` / `M.restrict U.ι` carrier distinction in the chart presentation prose matches the
  Lean `chartPresentation` signature (`e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ unit …` → `(M.over U).Presentation`).
- LaTeX: anonymous-constructor glyphs written as `\langle\langle\dots\rangle\rangle`; underscore in
  `chartPresentation\_isFinite` escaped.
